---
name: Proposal
about: Change what the standard says — new section, reframed argument, status promotion, new uncertainty
title: "Proposal: <one-line summary>"
labels: proposal
---

## What should the standard say (that it doesn't), or stop saying?

<!-- One paragraph. State the change, not the wording. -->

## Why

<!-- The argument. For new claims: what evidence supports it.
     For removals: what changed since it was written. -->

## Which pages does this touch?

<!-- List pages and sections. A proposal touching the manifesto or an
     uncertainty gets a higher bar than one touching a layer page. -->

## Does this change any commitment or uncertainty?

<!-- The manifesto's commitments (§6) and the uncertainties register are the
     document's load-bearing promises. Say explicitly if this strengthens,
     weakens, or resolves one — and on what evidence. -->

## Status implications

<!-- Does this promote a page (draft → settled) or demote one?
     Promotion proposals: link the page's open corrections (should be none). -->
